Around 90% of electronic equipment is recyclable. Our authorized programme with Deshwal Waste Management takes back end-of-life electronics anywhere in India — at zero cost to you.
Dumped in a landfill, old electronics leach lead and mercury into soil and groundwater. Recycled right, almost all of it

E-waste is the informal name for electronic products nearing the end of their useful life — computers, televisions, displays, copiers, phones, audio equipment and batteries. Many of their components contain hazardous materials that pose a real threat to human health and the environment when disposed of improperly.

Most of these products can be reused, refurbished or recycled in an environmentally sound manner. Under India's E-Waste (Management & Handling) Rules, Mindstec runs a compliant take-back programme so the hardware we distribute never ends up where it shouldn't.
s E-Waste (Management & Handling) Rules, Mindstec runs a compliant take-back programme so the hardware we distribute never ends up where it shouldn
Metals recovered from circuit boards, and the plastics and glass in monitors and televisions, go into new products — reducing the need to mine new raw materials.
Donated electronics become refurbished computers and phones for low-income families, schools and non-profits — access to technology they couldn't otherwise afford.
With around 90% of electronic equipment recyclable, demand for recycling builds new firms and new jobs — and a second market for the recovered materials.
CRTs and monitors contain lead; circuit boards carry cadmium, mercury and chromium. Safe recycling keeps these toxins out of trashcans, landfills and groundwater.
Check the product catalogue for end-of-life handling information before you dispose of anything.
Make sure only authorized recyclers and dismantlers handle your electronic products.
Call the toll-free line to dispose of products that have reached end of life.
Drop used electronics, batteries and accessories at your nearest authorized e-waste collection point.
Disconnect batteries and protect any glass surfaces against breakage before handing equipment over.
Dismantle electronic products on your own.
Throw electronics into bins marked with the "Do not dispose" sign.
Hand e-waste to informal, unorganized collectors like local scrap dealers or rag pickers.
Mix electronics with municipal garbage that ultimately reaches landfills.
A close-up of an electronic circuit board with gold and green traces
In India, most e-waste ends up in the informal sector, recycled with no consideration for health or the environment. Abiding by all pertinent e-waste laws, Mindstec has partnered with
— a recycler authorized by the appropriate government agencies — to provide drop-off centres and environmentally sound processing of end-of-life electronics.
Call the toll-free line 1800 102 9077, Monday to Friday, 10:00 AM – 5:30 PM — or drop your e-waste at any collection centre below.
Deshwal Waste Management, our authorized recycler, collects the equipment and transports it to the nearest collection centre.
Equipment is processed at government-authorized facilities in compliance with India's E-Waste Management & Handling Rules.
No fee is charged for giving goods for recycling, and no monetary benefit is offered in return — the sole aim of the programme is to keep the environment clean.
